title: urtica dioica subsp. pubescens
intro: Urtica dioica subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy stinging nettle, is a subspecies of the Urtica dioica plant that is characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Urtica dioica subsp. pubescens include relief from arthritis, improved digestion, and a reduced risk of urinary tract infections, among others. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs in herbal remedies.
